NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David when Nathan the prophet went to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Have mercy upon me, O God, According to Your lovingkindness; According to the multitude of Your tender mercies, Blot out my transgressions.
NKJV.2: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, And cleanse me from my sin.
NKJV.3: For I acknowledge my transgressions, And my sin is always before me.
NKJV.4: Against You, You only, have I sinned, And done this evil in Your sight— That You may be found just when You speak, And blameless when You judge.
NKJV.5: Behold, I was brought forth in iniquity, And in sin my mother conceived me.
NKJV.6: Behold, You desire truth in the inward parts, And in the hidden part You will make me to know wisdom.
NKJV.7: Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; Wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
NKJV.8: Make me hear joy and gladness, That the bones You have broken may rejoice.
NKJV.9: Hide Your face from my sins, And blot out all my iniquities.
NKJV.10: Create in me a clean heart, O God, And renew a steadfast spirit within me.
NKJV.11: Do not cast me away from Your presence, And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
NKJV.12: Restore to me the joy of Your salvation, And uphold me by Your generous Spirit.
NKJV.13: Then I will teach transgressors Your ways, And sinners shall be converted to You.
NKJV.14: Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God, The God of my salvation, And my tongue shall sing aloud of Your righteousness.
NKJV.15: O Lord, open my lips, And my mouth shall show forth Your praise.
NKJV.16: For You do not desire sacrifice, or else I would give it; You do not delight in burnt offering.
NKJV.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit, A broken and a contrite heart— These, O God, You will not despise.
NKJV.18: Do good in Your good pleasure to Zion; Build the walls of Jerusalem.
NKJV.19: Then You shall be pleased with the sacrifices of righteousness, With burnt offering and whole burnt offering; Then they shall offer bulls on Your altar.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>, when Nathan<S>5416</S> the prophet<S>5030</S> came<S>935</S> unto him, after he had gone in<S>935</S> to Bathsheba<S>1339</S>. Have mercy<S>2603</S> upon me, O God<S>430</S>, according to thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S>: according unto the multitude<S>7230</S> of thy tender mercies<S>7356</S> blot out<S>4229</S> my transgressions<S>6588</S>.

KJV.7: Purge<S>2398</S> me with hyssop<S>231</S>, and I shall be clean<S>2891</S>: wash<S>3526</S> me, and I shall be whiter<S>3835</S> than snow<S>7950</S>.

KJV.11: Cast me not away<S>7993</S> from thy presence<S>6440</S>; and take<S>3947</S> not thy holy<S>6944</S> spirit<S>7307</S> from me.
KJV.12: Restore<S>7725</S> unto me the joy<S>8342</S> of thy salvation<S>3468</S>; and uphold<S>5564</S> me with thy free<S>5081</S> spirit<S>7307</S>.
KJV.13: Then will I teach<S>3925</S> transgressors<S>6586</S> thy ways<S>1870</S>; and sinners<S>2400</S> shall be converted<S>7725</S> unto thee.
KJV.14: Deliver<S>5337</S> me from bloodguiltiness<S>1818</S>, O God<S>430</S>, thou God<S>430</S> of my salvation<S>8668</S>: and my tongue<S>3956</S> shall sing aloud<S>7442</S> of thy righteousness<S>6666</S>. <sup>bloodguiltiness: Heb. bloods</sup>
KJV.15: O Lord<S>136</S>, open<S>6605</S> thou my lips<S>8193</S>; and my mouth<S>6310</S> shall shew forth<S>5046</S> thy praise<S>8416</S>.
KJV.16: For thou desirest<S>2654</S> not sacrifice<S>2077</S>; else would I give<S>5414</S> it: thou delightest<S>7521</S> not in burnt offering<S>5930</S>. <sup>else: or, that I should</sup>
KJV.17: The sacrifices<S>2077</S> of God<S>430</S> are a broken<S>7665</S> spirit<S>7307</S>: a broken<S>7665</S> and a contrite<S>1794</S> heart<S>3820</S>, O God<S>430</S>, thou wilt not despise<S>959</S>.

KJV.19: Then shalt thou be pleased<S>2654</S> with the sacrifices<S>2077</S> of righteousness<S>6664</S>, with burnt offering<S>5930</S> and whole<S>3632</S> burnt offering<S>5930</S>: then shall they offer<S>5927</S> bullocks<S>6499</S> upon thine altar<S>4196</S>.

ESV.1: Have mercy on me,  O God, according to your steadfast love; according to your abundant mercy blot out my transgressions.
ESV.2: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin!
ESV.3: For I know my transgressions, and my sin is ever before me.
ESV.4: Against you, you only, have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight, so that you may be justified in your words and blameless in your judgment.
ESV.5: Behold, I was brought forth in iniquity, and in sin did my mother conceive me.
ESV.6: Behold, you delight in truth in the inward being, and you teach me wisdom in the secret heart.
ESV.7: Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
ESV.8: Let me hear joy and gladness; let the bones that you have broken rejoice.
ESV.9: Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.
ESV.10: Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right  spirit within me.
ESV.11: Cast me not away from your presence, and take not your Holy Spirit from me.
ESV.12: Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and uphold me with a willing spirit.
ESV.13: Then I will teach transgressors your ways, and sinners will return to you.
ESV.14: Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, O God of my salvation, and my tongue will sing aloud of your righteousness.
ESV.15: O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will declare your praise.
ESV.16: For you will not delight in sacrifice, or I would give it; you will not be pleased with a burnt offering.
ESV.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
ESV.18: Do good to Zion in your good pleasure; build up the walls of Jerusalem;
ESV.19: then will you delight in right sacrifices, in burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings; then bulls will be offered on your altar.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David, when Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Be gracious to me, O God, according to Your lovingkindness; According to the greatness of Your compassion blot out my transgressions.
NASB.2: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity And cleanse me from my sin.
NASB.3: For I know my transgressions, And my sin is ever before me.
NASB.4: Against You, You only, I have sinned And done what is evil in Your sight, So that You are justified when You speak And blameless when You judge.
NASB.5: Behold, I was brought forth in iniquity, And in sin my mother conceived me.
NASB.6: Behold, You desire truth in the innermost being, And in the hidden part You will make me know wisdom.
NASB.7: Purify me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; Wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
NASB.8: Make me to hear joy and gladness, Let the bones which You have broken rejoice.
NASB.9: Hide Your face from my sins And blot out all my iniquities.
NASB.10: Create in me a clean heart, O God, And renew a steadfast spirit within me.
NASB.11: Do not cast me away from Your presence And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
NASB.12: Restore to me the joy of Your salvation And sustain me with a willing spirit.
NASB.13: [Then] I will teach transgressors Your ways, And sinners will be converted to You.
NASB.14: Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, the God of my salvation; [Then] my tongue will joyfully sing of Your righteousness.
NASB.15: O Lord, open my lips, That my mouth may declare Your praise.
NASB.16: For You do not delight in sacrifice, otherwise I would give it; You are not pleased with burnt offering.
NASB.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; A broken and a contrite heart, O God, You will not despise.
NASB.18: By Your favor do good to Zion; Build the walls of Jerusalem.
NASB.19: Then You will delight in righteous sacrifices, In burnt offering and whole burnt offering; Then young bulls will be offered on Your altar.

NLT.1:
Have mercy on me, O God,
because of your unfailing love.
Because of your great compassion,
blot out the stain of my sins.
NLT.2:
Wash me clean from my guilt.
Purify me from my sin.
NLT.3:
For I recognize my rebellion;
it haunts me day and night.
NLT.4:
Against you, and you alone, have I sinned;
I have done what is evil in your sight.
You will be proved right in what you say,
and your judgment against me is just.
NLT.5:
For I was born a sinner —
yes, from the moment my mother conceived me.
NLT.6:
But you desire honesty from the womb,
teaching me wisdom even there.
NLT.7:
Purify me from my sins, and I will be clean;
wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
NLT.8:
Oh, give me back my joy again;
you have broken me —
now let me rejoice.
NLT.9:
Don’t keep looking at my sins.
Remove the stain of my guilt.
NLT.10:
Create in me a clean heart, O God.
Renew a loyal spirit within me.
NLT.11:
Do not banish me from your presence,
and don’t take your Holy Spirit from me.
NLT.12:
Restore to me the joy of your salvation,
and make me willing to obey you.
NLT.13:
Then I will teach your ways to rebels,
and they will return to you.
NLT.14:
Forgive me for shedding blood, O God who saves;
then I will joyfully sing of your forgiveness.
NLT.15:
Unseal my lips, O Lord,
that my mouth may praise you.
NLT.16:
You do not desire a sacrifice, or I would offer one.
You do not want a burnt offering.
NLT.17:
The sacrifice you desire is a broken spirit.
You will not reject a broken and repentant heart, O God.
NLT.18:
Look with favor on Zion and help her;
rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
NLT.19:
Then you will be pleased with sacrifices offered in the right spirit —
with burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings.
Then bulls will again be sacrificed on your altar.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David, when Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Have mercy on me, God, according to your loving kindness. According to the multitude of your tender mercies, blot out my transgressions.
WEB.2: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity. Cleanse me from my sin.
WEB.3: For I know my transgressions. My sin is constantly before me.
WEB.4: Against you, and you only, I have sinned, and done that which is evil in your sight; that you may be proved right when you speak, and justified when you judge.
WEB.5: Behold, I was born in iniquity. In sin my mother conceived me.
WEB.6: Behold, you desire truth in the inward parts. You teach me wisdom in the inmost place.
WEB.7: Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean. Wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
WEB.8: Let me hear joy and gladness, That the bones which you have broken may rejoice.
WEB.9: Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all of my iniquities.
WEB.10: Create in me a clean heart, O God. Renew a right spirit within me.
WEB.11: Don’t throw me from your presence, and don’t take your holy Spirit from me.
WEB.12: Restore to me the joy of your salvation. Uphold me with a willing spirit.
WEB.13: Then I will teach transgressors your ways. Sinners shall be converted to you.
WEB.14: Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God, the God of my salvation. My tongue shall sing aloud of your righteousness.
WEB.15: Lord, open my lips. My mouth shall declare your praise.
WEB.16: For you don’t delight in sacrifice, or else I would give it. You have no pleasure in burnt offering.
WEB.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit. A broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
WEB.18: Do well in your good pleasure to Zion. Build the walls of Jerusalem.
WEB.19: Then you will delight in the sacrifices of righteousness, in burnt offerings and in whole burnt offerings. Then they will offer bulls on your altar.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David, when the prophet Nathan came to him after he had gone to Bathsheba. ⓜ [1] Be gracious to me, God,
according to your faithful love;
according to your abundant compassion,
blot out my rebellion. ⓝ
CSB17.2:
Completely wash away my guilt
and cleanse me from my sin. ⓐ
CSB17.3:
For I am conscious of my rebellion,
and my sin is always before me. ⓑ
CSB17.4:
Against you — you alone — I have sinned
and done this evil in your sight. ⓒ
So you are right when you pass sentence;
you are blameless when you judge. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
Indeed, I was guilty when I was born;
I was sinful when my mother conceived me. ⓔ
CSB17.6:
Surely you desire integrity in the inner self,
and you teach me wisdom deep within. ⓕ
CSB17.7:
Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean;
wash me, and I will be whiter than snow. ⓖ
CSB17.8:
Let me hear joy and gladness;
let the bones you have crushed rejoice. ⓗ
CSB17.9:
Turn your face away [1] from my sins
and blot out all my guilt. ⓘ
CSB17.10:
God, create a clean heart for me
and renew a steadfast [2] spirit within me. ⓙ
CSB17.11:
Do not banish me from your presence
or take your Holy Spirit from me. ⓚ
CSB17.12:
Restore the joy of your salvation to me,
and sustain me by giving me a willing spirit. ⓛ
CSB17.13:
Then I will teach the rebellious your ways,
and sinners will return to you. ⓜ
CSB17.14:
Save me from the guilt of bloodshed, God — ⓝ
God of my salvation —
and my tongue will sing of your righteousness. ⓐ
CSB17.15:
Lord, open my lips,
and my mouth will declare your praise. ⓑ
CSB17.16:
You do not want a sacrifice, or I would give it;
you are not pleased with a burnt offering. ⓒ
CSB17.17:
The sacrifice pleasing to God is [3] a broken spirit.
You will not despise a broken and humbled heart, God. ⓓ
CSB17.18:
In your good pleasure, cause Zion to prosper;
build the walls of Jerusalem. ⓔ
CSB17.19:
Then you will delight in righteous sacrifices,
whole burnt offerings;
then bulls will be offered on your altar. ⓕ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David; when Nathan the prophet came to him after he had sinned with Bathsheba. Have mercy on me, O God, according to Your lovingkindness;
According to the greatness of Your compassion blot out my transgressions.
AMP.2:
Wash me thoroughly from my wickedness and guilt
And cleanse me from my sin.
AMP.3:
For I am conscious of my transgressions and I acknowledge them;
My sin is always before me.
AMP.4:
Against You, You only, have I sinned
And done that which is evil in Your sight,
So that You are justified when You speak [Your sentence]
And faultless in Your judgment.
AMP.5:
I was brought forth in [a state of] wickedness;
In sin my mother conceived me [and from my beginning I, too, was sinful].
AMP.6:
Behold, You desire truth in the innermost being,
And in the hidden part [of my heart] You will make me know wisdom.
AMP.7:
Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean;
Wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
AMP.8:
Make me hear joy and gladness and be satisfied;
Let the bones which You have broken rejoice.
AMP.9:
Hide Your face from my sins
And blot out all my iniquities.
AMP.10:
Create in me a clean heart, O God,
And renew a right and steadfast spirit within me.
AMP.11:
Do not cast me away from Your presence
And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
AMP.12:
Restore to me the joy of Your salvation
And sustain me with a willing spirit.
AMP.13:
Then I will teach transgressors Your ways,
And sinners shall be converted and return to You.
AMP.14:
Rescue me from bloodguiltiness, O God, the God of my salvation;
Then my tongue will sing joyfully of Your righteousness and Your justice.
AMP.15:
O Lord, open my lips,
That my mouth may declare Your praise.
AMP.16:
For You do not delight in sacrifice, or else I would give it;
You are not pleased with burnt offering.
AMP.17:
My [only] sacrifice [acceptable] to God is a broken spirit;
A broken and contrite heart [broken with sorrow for sin, thoroughly penitent], such, O God, You will not despise.
AMP.18:
By Your favor do good to Zion;
May You rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
AMP.19:
Then will You delight in the sacrifices of righteousness,
In burnt offering and whole burnt offering;
Then young bulls will be offered on Your altar.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, after he was confronted by Nathan about the affair with Bathsheba.
Generous in love—God, give grace!
Huge in mercy—wipe out my bad record.
MSG.2:
Scrub away my guilt,
soak out my sins in your laundry.
MSG.3:
I know how bad I've been;
my sins are staring me down.
MSG.4:
You're the One I've violated, and you've seen
it all, seen the full extent of my evil.
You have all the facts before you;
whatever you decide about me is fair.
MSG.5:
I've been out of step with you for a long time,
in the wrong since before I was born.
MSG.6:
What you're after is truth from the inside out.
Enter me, then; conceive a new, true life.
MSG.7:
Soak me in your laundry and I'll come out clean,
scrub me and I'll have a snow-white life.
MSG.8:
Tune me in to foot-tapping songs,
set these once-broken bones to dancing.
MSG.9:
Don't look too close for blemishes,
give me a clean bill of health.
MSG.10:
God, make a fresh start in me,
shape a Genesis week from the chaos of my life.
MSG.11:
Don't throw me out with the trash,
or fail to breathe holiness in me.
MSG.12:
Bring me back from gray exile,
put a fresh wind in my sails!
MSG.13:
Give me a job teaching rebels your ways
so the lost can find their way home.
MSG.14:
Commute my death sentence, God, my salvation God,
and I'll sing anthems to your life-giving ways.
MSG.15:
Unbutton my lips, dear God;
I'll let loose with your praise.
MSG.16:
Going through the motions doesn't please you,
a flawless performance is nothing to you.
MSG.17:
I learned God-worship
when my pride was shattered.
Heart-shattered lives ready for love
don't for a moment escape God's notice.
MSG.18:
Make Zion the place you delight in,
repair Jerusalem's broken-down walls.
MSG.19:
Then you'll get real worship from us,
acts of worship small and large,
Including all the bulls
they can heave onto your altar!

NIV.1:
Psalm 51
For the director of music. A psalm of David. When the prophet Nathan came to him after David had committed adultery with Bathsheba.
Have mercy on me, O God,
according to your unfailing love;
according to your great compassion
blot out my transgressions.
NIV.2:
Wash away all my iniquity
and cleanse me from my sin.
NIV.3:
For I know my transgressions,
and my sin is always before me.
NIV.4:
Against you, you only, have I sinned
and done what is evil in your sight,
so that you are proved right when you speak
and justified when you judge.
NIV.5:
Surely I was sinful at birth,
sinful from the time my mother conceived me.
NIV.6:
Surely you desire truth in the inner parts;
you teach me wisdom in the inmost place.
NIV.7:
Cleanse me with hyssop, and I will be clean;
wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
NIV.8:
Let me hear joy and gladness;
let the bones you have crushed rejoice.
NIV.9:
Hide your face from my sins
and blot out all my iniquity.
NIV.10:
Create in me a pure heart, O God,
and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
NIV.11:
Do not cast me from your presence
or take your Holy Spirit from me.
NIV.12:
Restore to me the joy of your salvation
and grant me a willing spirit, to sustain me.
NIV.13:
Then I will teach transgressors your ways,
and sinners will turn back to you.
NIV.14:
Save me from bloodguilt, O God,
the God who saves me,
and my tongue will sing of your righteousness.
NIV.15:
O Lord, open my lips,
and my mouth will declare your praise.
NIV.16:
You do not delight in sacrifice, or I would bring it;
you do not take pleasure in burnt offerings.
NIV.17:
The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit;
a broken and contrite heart,
O God, you will not despise.
NIV.18:
In your good pleasure make Zion prosper;
build up the walls of Jerusalem.
NIV.19:
Then there will be righteous sacrifices,
whole burnt offerings to delight you;
then bulls will be offered on your altar.

NRSVCE.1:
Have mercy on me, O God,
according to your steadfast love;
according to your abundant mercy
blot out my transgressions.
NRSVCE.2:
Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity,
and cleanse me from my sin.
NRSVCE.3:
For I know my transgressions,
and my sin is ever before me.
NRSVCE.4:
Against you, you alone, have I sinned,
and done what is evil in your sight,
so that you are justified in your sentence
and blameless when you pass judgment.
NRSVCE.5:
Indeed, I was born guilty,
a sinner when my mother conceived me.
NRSVCE.6:
You desire truth in the inward being;
therefore teach me wisdom in my secret heart.
NRSVCE.7:
Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean;
wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
NRSVCE.8:
Let me hear joy and gladness;
let the bones that you have crushed rejoice.
NRSVCE.9:
Hide your face from my sins,
and blot out all my iniquities.
NRSVCE.10:
Create in me a clean heart, O God,
and put a new and right spirit within me.
NRSVCE.11:
Do not cast me away from your presence,
and do not take your holy spirit from me.
NRSVCE.12:
Restore to me the joy of your salvation,
and sustain in me a willing spirit.
NRSVCE.13:
Then I will teach transgressors your ways,
and sinners will return to you.
NRSVCE.14:
Deliver me from bloodshed, O God,
O God of my salvation,
and my tongue will sing aloud of your deliverance.
NRSVCE.15:
O Lord, open my lips,
and my mouth will declare your praise.
NRSVCE.16:
For you have no delight in sacrifice;
if I were to give a burnt offering, you would not be pleased.
NRSVCE.17:
The sacrifice acceptable to God is a broken spirit;
a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
NRSVCE.18:
Do good to Zion in your good pleasure;
rebuild the walls of Jerusalem,
NRSVCE.19:
then you will delight in right sacrifices,
in burnt offerings and whole burnt offerings;
then bulls will be offered on your altar.

NET.1: Have mercy on me, O God, because of your loyal love! Because of your great compassion, wipe away my rebellious acts!
NET.2: Wash away my wrongdoing! Cleanse me of my sin!
NET.3: For I am aware of my rebellious acts; I am forever conscious of my sin.
NET.4: Against you — you above all — I have sinned; I have done what is evil in your sight. So you are just when you confront me; you are right when you condemn me.
NET.5: Look, I was guilty of sin from birth, a sinner the moment my mother conceived me.
NET.6: Look, you desire integrity in the inner man; you want me to possess wisdom.
NET.7: Sprinkle me with water and I will be pure; wash me and I will be whiter than snow.
NET.8: Grant me the ultimate joy of being forgiven! May the bones you crushed rejoice!
NET.9: Hide your face from my sins! Wipe away all my guilt!
NET.10: Create for me a pure heart, O God! Renew a resolute spirit within me!
NET.11: Do not reject me! Do not take your Holy Spirit away from me!
NET.12: Let me again experience the joy of your deliverance! Sustain me by giving me the desire to obey!
NET.13: Then I will teach rebels your merciful ways, and sinners will turn to you.
NET.14: Rescue me from the guilt of murder, O God, the God who delivers me! Then my tongue will shout for joy because of your deliverance.
NET.15: O Lord, give me the words! Then my mouth will praise you.
NET.16: Certainly you do not want a sacrifice, or else I would offer it; you do not desire a burnt sacrifice.
NET.17: The sacrifices God desires are a humble spirit — O God, a humble and repentant heart you will not reject.
NET.18: Because you favor Zion, do what is good for her! Fortify the walls of Jerusalem!
NET.19: Then you will accept the proper sacrifices, burnt sacrifices and whole offerings; then bulls will be sacrificed on your altar.
